content.text: A **Multilingual AI Chatbot** based ticketing system built for Smart India Hackathon 2024. The system simplifies museum ticket booking for diverse linguistic communities across India, eliminating manual form filling and reducing operational overhead.

content.title: Hackathon Details
content.text: **Problem Statement ID** - 1648 | **Theme** - Travel & Tourism | **Team** - Code Monkeys (ID: 1363)

Museum ticketing in India suffers from long queues, complex booking forms, and a lack of support for regional languages. Over **90% of Indian internet users** prefer services in their local language, yet most ticketing platforms are English-only.

content.title: Core Pain Points
content.text: Average booking time is **10 minutes** per user. Ticketing errors like double bookings are common. Staff costs for manual ticketing are high. No multilingual support for diverse visitors.
Development of a **Multilingual AI Chatbot** based Ticketing System specialized for diverse linguistic communities. The chatbot handles the entire booking flow conversationally — no forms, no queues.

items: **Language Detection**: Automatically identifies and switches to the user's preferred language.
**No Form Filling**: Chatbot collects booking details conversationally and fills forms in the backend.
**Secure Payment Gateway**: Supports UPI, Credit/Debit cards, and Netbanking via Razorpay.
**Automated Email Confirmation**: Sends tickets and transaction details in the user's preferred language.
**Real Time Analytics**: Tracks user behavior and trends to optimize services.
Uses **Meta LLaMA** — a free and open source AI model, keeping costs minimal.
Completely **form-free booking** experience via natural conversation.
**Dynamic pricing** support based on time of day and special events.
**Membership programs** for frequent visitors with exclusive discounts.

content.title: Impact Numbers
content.text: **80%** reduction in ticketing errors · **75%** faster booking (10 min → 2.5 min) · **50%** savings on operational costs

content.caption: Figure 1: Full system architecture including Auth, Booking, Payment and Notification flows.

items: User submits credentials → Server encrypts using **Bcrypt**.
Server validates and issues a **JSON Web Token (JWT)**.
JWT is returned to the client for all further authenticated requests.
Hashed passwords and session IDs stored securely in MySQL.
User chats with AI Chatbot → Chatbot extracts booking intent and details.
Chatbot sends a structured **booking request to the server**.
Server processes request → redirects user to **Razorpay payment gateway**.
On payment success → **Email + SMS confirmation** sent to user in preferred language.
User requests cancellation via chatbot.
Chatbot sends **cancellation request to server**.
Server processes refund and updates the database.
Confirmation sent to user via email and SMS.

content.title: Microservices
content.text: Each service (Auth, Booking, Payment, Notifications, Analytics) is independently deployable on GCP, allowing targeted scaling during peak hours.

items: **React JS** - Component-based UI for the booking interface.
**Tailwind CSS** - Utility-first styling for responsive design.
**Bootstrap** - Additional UI component support.
**Node JS + Express JS** - Core server and REST API layer.
**MySQL** - Relational database for bookings, users, and transactions.
**Python** - AI model integration and chatbot inference layer.
**Google Cloud Platform** - Microservices architecture deployment.
**Google Cloud E2 Medium Instance** - Primary compute.
**Cloud SQL** - Managed MySQL database on GCP.
**Razorpay** - Payment gateway with UPI, cards, and netbanking.
**Google Translation API** - Multilingual support for 20+ Indian languages.
**Google Analytics** - User behavior and funnel tracking.
**Mailgun** - Transactional email for booking confirmations.
**Textlocal API** - SMS notifications.

content.title: AI Model
content.text: The chatbot runs on **Meta LLaMA 3**, hosted locally on the server. This eliminates per-token API costs and keeps all user data on-premise.

content.code:
# Authentication
POST /api/auth/register     → Register new user
POST /api/auth/login        → Login, returns JWT

# Chatbot
POST /api/chat              → Send message, get chatbot response

# Bookings
POST /api/booking/create    → Create new booking
GET  /api/booking/:id       → Get booking details
DELETE /api/booking/:id     → Cancel booking

# Payments
POST /api/payment/init      → Initialize Razorpay order
POST /api/payment/verify    → Verify payment signature

items: **Google Cloud E2 Medium Instance** - ₹15,000/month
**Cloud SQL Database** - ₹8,000/month
**Meta LLaMA (AI Model)** - ₹0 (runs locally)
**SMS via TextLocal** - ₹2,950 per 10,000 SMS
**Domain + Mailbox (Hostinger)** - ₹750 + ₹1,450
**Mailgun Email** - ₹1,300/month + ₹150 per 1,000 emails
**ChatGPT + AWS + APIs** - ₹80,000/month
**Gemini + Google Cloud + APIs** - ₹70,000/month
**Vertex AI + AWS + APIs** - ₹65,000/month
**LLaMA + Google Cloud + APIs (Our Stack)** - ₹30,000/month ✅

content.title: Cost Advantage
content.text: By using **Meta LLaMA locally**, we achieve a **62% cost reduction** compared to ChatGPT-based alternatives, without compromising on AI quality.
**Challenge**: Training LLaMA with multiple Indian languages is resource-intensive and time-consuming.
**Mitigation**: Use **Google Translation API** to handle language translation as a middleware layer, keeping the core LLaMA model language-agnostic.
**Challenge**: Museum events and holidays can cause sudden traffic spikes.
**Mitigation**: GCP's **auto-scaling microservices** architecture allows independent scaling of the booking and payment services during peak hours.

content.title: Data Privacy
content.text: All user PII is encrypted at rest using Bcrypt and AES-256. The LLaMA model runs locally — no user data is ever sent to third-party AI APIs.

items: **80%** reduction in ticketing errors such as double bookings or lost records.
**75%** faster booking — average time drops from 10 minutes to just 2.5 minutes.
**50%** savings on operational costs by reducing dedicated ticketing staff.
Reaches **90% of Indian internet users** who prefer services in their local language.
Dynamic pricing and membership programs drive visitor loyalty and repeat visits.

content.title: Social Impact
content.text: By supporting regional Indian languages, the system makes cultural institutions like museums accessible to first-time digital users from Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
